get.summary: GET /agents — list all loaded agents with full details (truncated system prompts).

200.description: List of agents with full details

400.description: Invalid tool or MCP server name

get.summary: List all pending approval requests for a conversation.

post.summary: Bulk resolve multiple pending approval requests.
Resolve a single pending approval request.
POST /conversations/:conv_id/abort — abort the current in-flight turn.
POST /conversations/:conv_id/messages — send a message to a conversation.

get.summary: GET /conversations/:conv_id/stream — SSE stream for a conversation.
GET /metrics — collect and return metrics from all agents.

put.summary: PUT /push/agents/{agent_id} — add if new, update if version differs, no-op if same version.

post.summary: POST /push/agents/{agent_id}/conversations — hydrate conversations for an agent.
POST /push/diff — apply a diff of agent changes.

AbortConversationResponse.description: Response for aborting a conversation turn.

disabled_tools.description:
Tools to disable for this agent. Takes priority over everything else —
disabled tools are removed from the registry before the agent is built,
so the LLM never sees them. Works for built-in tools, MCP tools,
integration tools, and spider tools.

oneOf.description:
Strip tool-result bodies from old messages before sending history to the
LLM. Full output already lives on disk via the spill pipeline; the
stripped message keeps a pointer so the agent can read it via RipGrep
if needed. Omit to use defaults (stripping enabled). Set `enabled:
false` to turn off entirely.

Immortal conversation configuration (chain-based context management).
When set, replaces compaction — the conversation chains into fresh context
windows transparently while maintaining a living journal.

max_concurrent_conversations.description:
Maximum concurrent conversations for this specific agent.
Takes precedence over the global max_concurrent_conversations.

max_tasks_per_conversation.description:
Maximum total subagent tasks per conversation. Limits resource consumption
from recursive/parallel subagent spawning. Default: 50.

rate_limit_rpm.description: Rate limit in requests per minute

subagent_timeout_background_secs.description:
Wall-clock timeout (seconds) applied when this agent is invoked as a
background subagent. Default: 300 (5 minutes).

subagent_timeout_foreground_secs.description:
Wall-clock timeout (seconds) applied when this agent is invoked as a
foreground subagent. Default: 300 (5 minutes).

tool_calls_only.description:
When true, the agent can complete a turn with only tool calls and no text.
Empty text responses are treated as success if tool calls were executed.
Default: false.

tool_requirements.description:
Declarative tool-call requirements evaluated at the end of every agent
turn. Each entry describes a tool that must be called (with optional
cadence, position, and min-call constraints) and what bridge should do
if the requirement is violated. See [`ToolRequirement`] for the shape
and [`RequirementEnforcement`] for the dispatch options.

Default: empty (no enforcement).

AgentDefinition.description: Complete definition of an AI agent fetched from the control plane.

description.description:
Human-readable description of the agent's purpose and capabilities.
Used in tool documentation when this agent is a subagent.

integrations.description:
External integrations (e.g., GitHub, Slack, Mailchimp).
Each integration's actions become individual tools for the agent.

permissions.description:
Per-tool permission overrides. Key = tool name, Value = permission level.
Tools not listed default to `Allow`.

updated_at.description: Last updated timestamp for change detection

version.description: Version field for change detection during sync

AgentResponse.description:
Full agent response — returned by both list and detail endpoints.
System prompts may be truncated on the list endpoint.

registered_tools.description: All tools registered and available to this agent (built-in + MCP + integrations).

AgentSummary.description: Lightweight agent summary for listing endpoints.

ApprovalDecision.description: The user's decision on an approval request.

ApprovalReply.description: HTTP request body for resolving a single approval.

reason.description:
Optional message explaining why the tool call was denied.
Included in the error returned to the LLM so the agent can adjust.

ApprovalRequest.description: A pending approval request for a tool call.

agent_id.description: Agent that initiated the tool call.

conversation_id.description: Conversation in which the tool call occurred.

created_at.description: When the approval request was created.

id.description: Unique ID for this approval request.

status.description: Current status of the approval request.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool being called.

BulkApprovalReply.description: HTTP request body for resolving multiple approvals at once.

reason.description: Optional message explaining why the tool calls were denied.

not_found.description: List of request IDs that were not found.

resolved.description: List of request IDs that were successfully resolved.

CacheTtl.description:
Prompt-cache TTL hint.

Anthropic supports two TTLs today: 5-minute ephemeral (default,
1.25× cache-write multiplier) and 1-hour extended (2.0× write, needs
`extended-cache-ttl-2025-04-11` beta header). Other providers ignore
this field — their caches are automatic and provider-managed.

**Note (rig 0.31)**: `with_prompt_caching()` only emits the
`{"type": "ephemeral"}` breakpoint, which corresponds to the 5-minute
TTL. Setting `OneHour` here sends the beta header but does not yet
add `"ttl": "1h"` to the cache_control block — upgrade rig to get
true 1-hour writes. The field is wired now so the choice survives
the upgrade.

summary_prompt.description: Custom system prompt for the summarization call. Uses a default if None.

summary_provider.description: Provider config for the summarization model (required — defined by control plane).

tail_messages.description: Number of recent messages to preserve verbatim after compaction.

token_budget.description: Token budget. Compaction triggers when estimated tokens exceed this.

ContentBlock.description: A content block within a message.

ConversationRecord.description: A conversation with its full message history, as returned by the control plane.

updated_at.description: When the conversation was last updated.

CreateConversationRequest.description: Optional request body for creating a conversation with tool/MCP scoping.

api_key.description:
When provided, overrides the agent's LLM API key for this conversation only.
For full provider/model override, use the `provider` field instead.

mcp_server_names.description:
When provided, only tools from these MCP servers are available.
Server names must match the agent's configured MCP server names.

mcp_servers.description:
Additional MCP servers to load for this conversation only.
Connected at conversation creation, torn down when the conversation ends
(or is aborted, drained, or cancelled). Tool names produced by these
servers must not collide with the agent's existing tool names.

Stdio transport requires the runtime config flag
`allow_stdio_mcp_from_api` to be enabled; otherwise only
`streamable_http` is accepted.

oneOf.description:
When provided, fully overrides the agent's LLM provider for this conversation.
Allows switching model, provider type, API key, and base URL per conversation
while keeping the same agent definition (tools, system prompt, skills, etc.).

subagent_api_keys.description:
Per-subagent API key overrides. Key = subagent name, Value = API key.
Only named subagents are overridden; others keep their configured keys.

tool_names.description:
When provided, only these tools are available in the conversation.
Tool names must match the agent's registered tool names exactly.

conversation_id.description: The ID of the newly created conversation.

stream_url.description: The URL to stream events from this conversation.

total_active_conversations.description: Total active conversations across all agents

status.description: Health status - always "ok" if the server is running.

HistoryStripConfig.description:
Configuration for stripping tool-result bodies from old messages before
they are sent to the LLM. Reduces input tokens while preserving the ability
to recover the full content via the on-disk spill file (`RipGrep` on the
spill path). Strip is applied at send-time only; persistence is untouched,
so the decision is deterministic across turns and the provider prompt
cache remains stable after a result is first stripped.

age_threshold.description:
Number of assistant messages that must follow a tool result before it
becomes eligible for stripping. Mirrors the "old tool output may be
cleared later" contract in Claude Code's system prompt.

enabled.description: Master switch. When false, strip is a no-op.

pin_errors.description:
When true, tool results with `is_error: true` are never stripped.
Error context is small and high-signal for future turns.

pin_recent_count.description:
Always keep the most recent N tool results regardless of age. Protects
results the agent is actively reasoning over on the current turn.

ImmortalConfig.description:
Configuration for immortal conversations (chain-based context management).

When the token budget is exceeded, instead of compacting (summarizing in-place),
Bridge extracts a structured checkpoint, resets the history with the checkpoint +
journal + recent turns, and continues seamlessly. The external conversation_id
never changes.

carry_forward_budget_fraction.description:
Fraction of `token_budget` allowed for the carry-forward tail.
Prevents a single tool-heavy turn from stuffing the new chain's context.
Default 0.3 (30%).

carry_forward_turns.description:
Upper bound on the number of recent user turns to carry forward verbatim
into the new chain. The carry-forward is further capped by
`carry_forward_budget_fraction` — whichever binds first wins.

checkpoint_max_tokens.description: Max tokens for each checkpoint LLM call's output. Default 1500.

checkpoint_prompt.description: Custom prompt for checkpoint extraction. Uses a built-in default if None.

checkpoint_provider.description: Provider config for the checkpoint extraction LLM call.

checkpoint_timeout_secs.description: Per-call timeout for checkpoint LLM calls, in seconds. Default 45.

max_previous_checkpoints.description:
Max number of prior chain checkpoints to include as context during
checkpoint extraction. Older ones are considered subsumed. Default 2.

token_budget.description: Token budget. Chain triggers when estimated tokens exceed this.

verify_checkpoint.description:
When true, run a second verification pass after the phase-1 extraction.
Default false — for strong summarizer models phase 2 rarely improves output.

IntegrationAction.description:
A single action within an integration that an agent can invoke.

Each action maps to a specific API operation on the external service.
The control plane defines the schema and permissions for each action.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this action does.

parameters_schema.description: JSON Schema for the action's parameters.

IntegrationDefinition.description:
Definition of an external integration available to an agent.

Each integration represents a connection to an external service (e.g., GitHub,
Mailchimp, Slack) managed by the control plane. The control plane handles
authentication and proxies requests to the underlying service.

McpServerDefinition.description: Definition of an MCP server that an agent connects to.

transport.description: Transport configuration for connecting to the server

oneOf.description: Stdio transport — spawns a child process
Streamable HTTP transport — connects to an HTTP endpoint

McpTransport.description: Transport configuration for MCP server connections.

system_reminder.description:
Optional per-message system reminder injected by the control plane.
Wrapped in `<system-reminder>` tags and prepended to the user message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the message was created

MetricsSnapshot.description: Serializable snapshot of agent metrics for the /metrics endpoint.

cached_input_tokens.description: Total input tokens served from the provider prompt cache (discounted).

input_tokens.description: Total non-cached input tokens consumed (full price).

total_tokens.description: Total tokens (fresh input + cached input + output)

cache_ttl.description: TTL for explicit cache breakpoints. See [`CacheTtl`].

prompt_caching_enabled.description:
Whether to enable explicit prompt-cache breakpoints for providers
that require them (Anthropic today; MiniMax's Anthropic-shape
endpoint tomorrow). Implicit-cache providers (OpenAI, GLM, Gemini
2.5 implicit, Kimi K2) ignore this — they cache unconditionally.

RegisteredToolSummary.description: Summary of a registered tool (built-in, MCP, or integration).

oneOf.description: Required only on the very first turn of the conversation.

Required whenever `n` turns have passed without the tool being called.
The counter resets any time the tool is called (on- or off-cycle).
So `n=3` means "never go more than 3 consecutive turns without
calling this tool" — useful for periodic memory-retain / checkpoint
patterns.

RequirementCadence.description: Describes which turns a tool requirement applies to.

RequirementEnforcement.description: How bridge reacts when a tool requirement is violated.

RequirementPosition.description: Where in the turn's tool-call sequence the required call must appear.

request_id.description: The ID of the resolved request.

Role.description: Role of a message sender in a conversation.

SendMessageRequest.description: Request body for creating a message.

content.description:
The text content to send. When [`full_message`](Self::full_message) is
also supplied, `content` is the LLM-visible summary; omit it to let
bridge auto-generate one from the first bytes of `full_message`.

full_message.description:
Optional full payload written to a per-conversation attachment file.
When present, bridge writes it to disk, appends a `<system-reminder>`
with the file path and tool-usage hint to `content`, and sends the
composed text to the LLM. Callers use this to offload large inputs
(stack traces, log dumps, file contents) without bloating the
agent's context on every turn.

Failures (disk full, permission denied) do NOT reject the message —
bridge logs a warning and delivers `content` alone.

system_reminder.description:
Optional system reminder to inject with this message.
Will be wrapped in `<system-reminder>` tags and prepended to the user message.

SkillDefinition.description: Definition of a skill that can be activated by an agent.

content.description:
Full skill prompt/instructions content (the SKILL.md body or single-content string).
Can contain template variables like {{args}}, $ARGUMENTS, $1, $2, etc.

description.description: Description of what the skill does

files.description:
Supporting files: relative_path -> file_content.
Contains files referenced by the skill (e.g., "api-reference.md", "examples/ex1.md").

oneOf.description: Parsed frontmatter configuration from SKILL.md YAML header.

parameters_schema.description: Optional JSON Schema for structured parameters (for future use)

SkillFrontmatter.description:
Frontmatter configuration parsed from a SKILL.md YAML header.

Only applicable to sources that support frontmatter (ClaudeCode, AgentSkills).

agent.description: Sub-agent type to delegate to when context is "fork".

allowed_tools.description: List of tools the skill is allowed to use.

argument_hint.description: Hint text shown for the argument placeholder in autocomplete.

context.description: Execution context. "fork" runs in an isolated subagent.

disable_model_invocation.description: When true, only users can invoke this skill (hidden from the model).

paths.description: Glob patterns for auto-activation on matching files.

shell.description: Shell to use for shell-related operations (e.g., "bash", "powershell").

user_invocable.description: When false, only the model can invoke this skill (hidden from slash commands).

registered_tools.description: All tools registered and available to this subagent at runtime.

ToolCall.description: A request to call a tool.

arguments.description: Arguments to pass to the tool

id.description: Unique identifier for this tool call

name.description: Name of the tool to call

ToolCallStatsSnapshot.description: Serializable snapshot of per-tool call statistics.

total_calls.description: Total number of calls to this tool

ToolDefinition.description: Definition of a tool that an agent can use.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the tool does

parameters_schema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's parameters

ToolPermission.description: Permission level for a tool within an agent.

ToolRequirement.description:
A single tool-call requirement that bridge enforces at turn boundaries.

Typical configurations:
- `journal_write` every turn: `{ tool: "journal_write" }` (all defaults).
- `memory_recall` at the start of every turn:
  `{ tool: "memory_recall", position: "turn_start" }`.
- `memory_retain` at most every 3 turns:
  `{ tool: "memory_retain", cadence: { type: "every_n_turns", n: 3 }, position: "turn_end" }`.

Tool-name matching is flexible to reduce MCP verbosity: if `tool` contains
`__`, match it verbatim; otherwise match any registered tool whose full
name equals `tool` OR ends with `__<tool>`. So `"post_message"` matches
an MCP tool exposed as `slack__post_message` without the user having to
write the server prefix.

cadence.description:
When this requirement applies (which turns must satisfy it).
Default: `EveryTurn`.

enforcement.description:
What bridge does when this requirement is violated.
Default: `NextTurnReminder` — attach a system reminder to the next
user message. Zero extra LLM cost this turn.

min_calls.description: Minimum number of calls required in a qualifying turn. Default: 1.

position.description:
Where in the turn the call must appear.
Default: `Anywhere`. Evaluation is LENIENT: read-only/metadata tools
(`todoread`, `journal_read`, `ls`, `read`, etc.) are exempt and do
not disqualify a `TurnStart` position requirement.

reminder_message.description:
Custom reminder text injected when the requirement is violated.
Falls back to a generated default when unset.

tool.description: Tool name to require (built-in, MCP, integration, or custom).

ToolResult.description: The result of a tool execution.

is_error.description: Whether the tool execution resulted in an error

tool_call_id.description: ID of the tool call this result corresponds to

UpdateApiKeyResponse.description: Response for updating an API key.
